title: lamium album var. barbatum
intro: Lamium album var. barbatum, also known as white deadnettle, is a perennial plant in the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its white or pale purple flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections. The health benefits of white deadnettle include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. White deadnettle can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in herbal remedies to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
